title: sagina decumbens subsp. occidentalis
intro: Sagina decumbens subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western Starwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Western Starwort is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
